claim: Cloudflare achieved $1 per AI code review by building a custom CI-native orchestration system with specialized reviewer agents rather than using out-of-the-box solutions
actionable: For cost-effective agentic systems at scale, build custom orchestration on top of open platforms rather than relying on generic out-of-box tools

claim: Multi-agent specialization (7 specialized reviewers: security, performance, code quality, documentation, release management, compliance) outperforms single monolithic code review agents
actionable: Structure code review agents as specialized domain experts coordinated by an orchestrator rather than asking one model to handle all concerns

claim: Orchestrating agents at different compute tiers based on risk level (trivial changes=2 agents, light changes=3-4 agents, complex changes=all specialists) preserves token economics
actionable: Don't deploy full agent teams for all tasks; implement risk-based routing to match compute intensity to actual complexity (lines changed, files affected)

claim: Using JSONL streaming with step_finish events enables real-time monitoring, cost tracking, error detection, and retry logic rather than waiting for complete JSON responses
actionable: Structure agent outputs as JSONL streams and attach listeners to step_finish events for real-time observability, error handling, and cost control

claim: Tightly scoped prompts specifying what agents should look for AND what to ignore reduces false positives and nitpicks compared to generic review prompts
actionable: When prompting agents, include explicit negative instructions (what NOT to flag) alongside positive instructions to reduce noise and false positives

claim: Sharing diff context across sub-agents rather than duplicating increases costs 7x; per-domain diff reading reduces token usage while maintaining accuracy
actionable: In multi-agent systems, establish shared context files and have each agent read only domain-relevant diffs rather than duplicating full context across agents

claim: Using model stacks (state-of-the-art for complex tasks, workhorse models for standard tasks, lightweight for simple tasks) is essential for sustainable token economics
actionable: Don't use your most expensive models for all tasks; implement routing logic that uses cheaper models for simpler tasks and reserves expensive models for complex decisions

claim: Biasing code review toward approval with warnings rather than hard blocks when production risk is low accelerates the system toward agent-forward operations
actionable: Configure your agentic systems to default to approval when clear production risks aren't present, reserving hard blocks for genuine critical issues

claim: Fallback mechanisms allowing humans to override agent decisions (break glass) and retain agent output for future learning prevents hard blocking but captures feedback
actionable: Always provide escape hatches for human override of agent decisions, and log disagreements as valuable training signal for system improvement

claim: Cloudflare's code review agents found 8,000 critical issues across 130,000 reviews, with security and code quality agents providing highest ROI on token spend
actionable: When building agentic systems, measure which specialized agents generate the most valuable findings and optimize resource allocation accordingly

claim: Plugin-based architecture with bootstrap-configure-post_configure lifecycle hooks enables quick system updates across 5,000+ codebases without monolithic changes
actionable: Design agent systems with extensible plugin patterns that implement consistent lifecycle hooks to enable rapid iteration and cross-project consistency
